steps.title: Recognize the appositive structure
steps.content: ”Created by Niklaus Wirth” is an appositive phrase—a non-essential explanation that provides more detail about Pascal. Appositives must be set off with matching punctuation marks on both sides.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: Choice B correctly uses paired commas to enclose the appositive: “Pascal, created by Niklaus Wirth,” and lets “for teaching programming skills” stand separately. Option A pairs em-dashes but groups two distinct information types together; options C and D lack or misuse punctuation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
